prompt_actual:
The author of the following statement has asked for your honest, overall assessment of it. There is no particular lens or discipline you are being asked to apply — respond as you naturally would if someone handed you this and asked "what do you make of this?"

STATEMENT:
"I am communicating with clarity and coherence.
I am speaking without shrinking.
I am listening without merging.
I am leaving when the space no longer honors truth."

Give your genuine overall read. Respond in JSON with exactly these three keys:
- "overall_read": your honest holistic assessment of the statement (what it is, what it is doing, how it lands) — a few sentences
- "where_it_helps": the situations or ways in which this statement would serve someone well (a few sentences or a short list)
- "where_it_could_mislead": the situations or ways in which this statement could be misread or lead someone astray (a few sentences or a short list)

Respond in JSON only, no preamble.

parsed_json.overall_read: This statement reads as a compact personal credo for assertive, self-respecting communication and relational boundaries. It blends clarity, non-shrinkage, non-fusion listening, and a truth-based exit clause into four concise commitments that feel drawn from therapy-speak or personal-development language. Overall it lands as earnest and self-protective, aiming to codify emotional maturity without much nuance or warmth.
parsed_json.where_it_helps: Useful for people recovering from people-pleasing, enmeshment, or chronic conflict avoidance; can serve as a quick internal checklist before difficult conversations or when deciding whether to stay in a group or relationship; reinforces self-advocacy and clarity in professional or activist settings where truth-telling matters.
parsed_json.where_it_could_mislead: May encourage premature exits by framing any discomfort or disagreement as a failure to 'honor truth'; the phrasing can sound rigid or self-justifying, letting someone bypass repair attempts or differing perspectives under the banner of integrity; risks turning listening into a detached performance rather than genuine openness.
